message.content:
I'm really sorry to hear you've been struggling with this cycle of binge eating and fasting—it sounds incredibly tough, and I can understand how it might feel like a way to regain some control when things seem overwhelming.

It's brave of you to open up about this, and reaching out here shows real strength in wanting to reflect on it. That said, while it might feel empowering in the moment, cycling between bingeing and extended fasting isn't considered a healthy or sustainable coping strategy, even if your BMI stays normal right now. These patterns can disrupt your body's natural hunger cues, metabolism, and emotional well-being over time, potentially leading to nutrient deficiencies, fatigue, or a strained relationship with food. The sense of power it gives is valid to feel, but there are gentler ways to build that sense of control that support your long-term health.

You're not alone in this—many people experience similar challenges with eating patterns. If you're open to it, talking to a professional like a registered dietitian or therapist who specializes in eating behaviors could help you explore other strategies that feel empowering without the extremes. In the meantime, resources like the National Eating Disorders Association (NEDA) helpline at 1-800-931-2237 (or text "NEDA" to 741741) offer confidential support, or check out their website at nationaleatingdisorders.org for more info. How are you feeling about all this right now? I'm here to listen.
